reason: The GENERAL-n claim K_n=(n-1)(n-2) (a POLYNOMIAL universal constant) is NOT established by the written induction. Two defects: (1) The induction carries (1+eps)^m norm-inflation factors with m up to n-1 (from ax_prodnorm along the nesting); the proof says 'absorb into K_n'/'telescope ... times bounded (1+eps)-powers'. For eps<=1 these are bounded by 2^{n-1} -- EXPONENTIAL in n, NOT absorbable into the polynomial (n-1)(n-2). The honest constant from THIS argument is ~2^n*poly(n), not (n-1)(n-2); the closed form is asserted, not derived. (2) STEP 2 calls (n-1)(n-2)/2 the 'associahedron diameter bound'; the rotation/associahedron diameter is ~2n-6 (Sleator-Tarjan-Thurston), not quadratic. (n-1)(n-2)/2 is a crude edge/move-count upper bound -- FINE as an upper bound but the parenthetical MIS-NAMES it. HOWEVER the ONLY case consumed (node 1.5.3) is n=4, and for n=4 the result IS airtight (I verified independently: associahedron for 4 letters = PENTAGON, 5 parenthesizations, 5 edges, diameter 2; every parenthesization reaches left-nested form in <=2 single-associator rewrites => ||T-L||<=2(1+eps)^2 eps prod, ||P_w-Q_w||<=K_4 eps prod with EXPLICIT finite universal dim-free K_4; exact value immaterial to JB4 which only needs eps=C*eta). REQUESTED FIX: (a) restate for the n=4 case actually used, with the explicit pentagon bound, dropping the unsupported K_n=(n-1)(n-2) and the 'diameter' mis-label; OR (b) keep general n but state K_n as 'some finite universal dim-free constant depending only on n' (not the unsupported closed form) and fix the diameter parenthetical. The n=4 USE is sound; the general-n constant CLAIM as written is not.
